verificationNote: AI-generated indicative risk register for Malaysian financial institutions under BNM RMiT. Risk scores are illustrative — organizations must calibrate to their own environment, threat landscape, and control maturity.
title: Technology Risk Register
description: Register of 20 technology risks specific to Malaysian financial institutions under BNM RMiT

risks.title: Ransomware Attack
risks.description: Encryption of critical banking systems and data by ransomware threat actors, potentially disrupting core banking operations and customer services nationwide.
existingControls: Endpoint detection and response (EDR) deployed on all endpoints
Network segmentation isolating core banking from general IT
Immutable offline backups with weekly restoration testing
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy deception technology, conduct quarterly ransomware tabletop exercises, and implement zero-trust network access for privileged systems.

risks.title: Core Banking System Outage
risks.description: Extended unplanned downtime of core banking platform affecting transaction processing, ATM services, and customer-facing channels.
existingControls: Active-active data centre configuration with automatic failover
24/7 NOC monitoring with defined SLAs and escalation procedures
Quarterly disaster recovery drills with documented RTO/RPO targets
risks.treatmentPlan: Upgrade to geo-redundant DR site, reduce RTO from 4 hours to 1 hour, and automate failover testing.

risks.title: Cloud Concentration Risk
risks.description: Over-reliance on a single cloud service provider creating systemic dependency, with potential for widespread service disruption if the provider experiences outages or exits the Malaysian market.
existingControls: Multi-cloud strategy with workloads distributed across two CSPs
Contractual exit clauses and data portability requirements
Annual cloud provider due diligence and BNM notification compliance
risks.treatmentPlan: Develop cloud exit playbook, maintain portable container-based architecture, and establish secondary CSP warm standby.

risks.title: Third-Party Vendor Failure
risks.description: Critical IT vendor (e.g., payment gateway, core banking provider) experiences financial distress, security breach, or service degradation impacting bank operations.
existingControls: Annual vendor risk assessments with financial health monitoring
Contractual SLAs with penalty clauses and right-to-audit provisions
Maintained list of pre-qualified alternative vendors for critical services
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement continuous vendor monitoring platform, require SOC 2 Type II from all critical vendors, and maintain tested transition plans.

risks.title: Insider Threat
risks.description: Malicious or negligent actions by employees, contractors, or privileged users leading to data exfiltration, fraud, or system sabotage.
existingControls: Privileged access management (PAM) with session recording
User behaviour analytics (UBA) monitoring anomalous access patterns
Background checks and periodic access recertification for all staff
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy data loss prevention (DLP) on all egress points, implement just-in-time access for production systems, and enhance exit procedures.

risks.title: Customer Data Breach
risks.description: Unauthorized access to or exfiltration of customer personal and financial data, triggering BNM notification requirements and reputational damage.
existingControls: Data classification and encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3)
Database activity monitoring with real-time alerting on bulk data access
Annual penetration testing by CREST-certified assessors
risks.treatmentPlan: Transfer residual financial exposure via comprehensive cyber insurance policy covering data breach costs, regulatory fines, and customer notification expenses. Retain third-party managed detection and response (MDR) service agreement for 24/7 breach monitoring and incident response.

risks.title: Change Management Failure
risks.description: Inadequate change management processes leading to production incidents, security vulnerabilities introduced through uncontrolled deployments, or system instability.
existingControls: Change Advisory Board (CAB) approval required for all production changes
Automated CI/CD pipeline with mandatory code review and testing gates
Post-implementation review (PIR) within 5 business days of deployment
risks.treatmentPlan: Residual risk within appetite after existing controls (CAB approval, CI/CD gates, PIR). Cost of further mitigation (canary deployments, extended freeze windows) exceeds potential impact given current low residual risk score.

risks.title: API Security Vulnerability
risks.description: Exploitation of insecure APIs in open banking, mobile banking, or third-party integrations leading to unauthorized transactions or data exposure.
existingControls: API gateway with rate limiting, authentication, and input validation
Quarterly API security assessments using OWASP API Top 10 methodology
OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect for all external API authentication
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy runtime API protection (RASP), implement mutual TLS for all partner APIs, and establish API inventory and lifecycle management.

risks.title: Mobile Banking Fraud
risks.description: Fraud through compromised mobile banking applications, SIM swap attacks, or social engineering targeting mobile banking customers.
existingControls: Multi-factor authentication with biometric and device binding
Real-time transaction monitoring with ML-based fraud detection
Device integrity checks and jailbreak/root detection
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement adaptive authentication based on risk scoring, deploy app shielding and obfuscation, and enhance customer fraud awareness campaigns.

risks.title: IT System Obsolescence
risks.description: Continued operation of end-of-life hardware, operating systems, or applications lacking vendor support and security patches.
existingControls: IT asset lifecycle management with EOL tracking and replacement budgets
Compensating controls (network isolation, IPS) for legacy systems awaiting migration
Annual technology currency assessment and board-reported modernization roadmap
risks.treatmentPlan: Accelerate legacy migration programme, implement virtual patching for unsupported systems, and allocate ring-fenced budget for technology refresh.

risks.title: DDoS Attack
risks.description: Volumetric or application-layer distributed denial of service attacks targeting internet-facing banking services, payment gateways, or API endpoints.
existingControls: Cloud-based DDoS mitigation service with auto-scaling scrubbing capacity
WAF and rate limiting on all internet-facing applications
DDoS response playbook with ISP coordination and traffic diversion procedures
risks.treatmentPlan: Engage secondary DDoS mitigation provider for redundancy, conduct semi-annual DDoS simulation exercises, and implement BGP Flowspec.

risks.title: Unauthorized Access to Critical Systems
risks.description: Compromise of privileged credentials or exploitation of access control weaknesses to gain unauthorized access to core banking, databases, or infrastructure.
existingControls: Multi-factor authentication enforced for all privileged and remote access
Privileged access management (PAM) vault with automated credential rotation
Quarterly access reviews with segregation of duties enforcement
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement zero-trust architecture with continuous verification, deploy FIDO2 hardware tokens for admin access, and establish breakglass procedures.

risks.title: Backup and Recovery Failure
risks.description: Inability to restore critical data or systems from backups due to corruption, incomplete backups, or untested recovery procedures.
existingControls: Automated backup verification with integrity checksums and alerts
Geographically separated backup storage with encryption
Monthly restoration testing of critical system backups with documented results
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement immutable backup architecture, automate full-stack recovery testing, and establish air-gapped backup copies for ransomware resilience.

risks.title: Regulatory Non-Compliance
risks.description: Failure to meet BNM RMiT requirements, PDPA obligations, or other regulatory mandates leading to enforcement action, fines, or operational restrictions.
existingControls: Dedicated compliance team tracking BNM directives and policy changes
Quarterly compliance self-assessments against RMiT requirements
Automated regulatory change monitoring and impact assessment process
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement GRC platform for continuous compliance monitoring, engage external advisors for annual RMiT gap analysis, and establish regulatory liaison programme.

risks.title: Supply Chain Compromise
risks.description: Compromise of software supply chain through malicious updates, tampered libraries, or backdoored vendor tools affecting bank systems.
existingControls: Software composition analysis (SCA) in CI/CD pipeline for all deployments
Vendor security assessment including SDLC review and code signing verification
Network monitoring for anomalous outbound connections from production systems
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement software bill of materials (SBOM) requirements for all vendors, deploy binary verification for all third-party components, and establish threat intelligence sharing with industry peers.

risks.title: AI/ML Model Risk
risks.description: Deployment of AI/ML models for credit scoring, fraud detection, or customer interactions with inadequate governance, bias, explainability, or adversarial robustness.
risks.rmitSection: S17 - Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
existingControls: AI governance framework with model risk management committee oversight
Mandatory bias testing and explainability documentation before deployment
Model performance monitoring with drift detection and automated alerts
risks.treatmentPlan: Avoid by prohibiting deployment of AI/ML models in regulated decision-making (credit scoring, customer-facing interactions) until governance framework is fully matured. Alternative rule-based approaches mandated for all regulated functions pending board-approved AI governance policy.

risks.title: Cryptographic Key Compromise
risks.description: Compromise or mismanagement of cryptographic keys used for data encryption, digital signatures, PKI, or HSM-protected payment processing.
existingControls: Hardware security modules (HSMs) for all production key storage and operations
Dual-control and split-knowledge key management procedures
Annual cryptographic assessment with algorithm currency review
risks.treatmentPlan: Develop post-quantum cryptography migration roadmap, implement automated key rotation, and enhance key ceremony audit trails.

risks.title: Shadow IT
risks.description: Use of unauthorized software, cloud services, or devices by business units outside IT governance, creating unmonitored attack surface and data leakage channels.
existingControls: Cloud access security broker (CASB) monitoring SaaS usage across the organization
IT procurement policy requiring CISO sign-off for all technology purchases
Quarterly shadow IT discovery scans and remediation campaigns
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated SaaS discovery and blocking, establish fast-track IT service request process, and enhance staff awareness on shadow IT risks.

risks.title: Social Engineering Attack
risks.description: Phishing, vishing, or business email compromise (BEC) targeting employees or customers to obtain credentials, initiate fraudulent transfers, or install malware.
existingControls: Email security gateway with advanced threat protection and URL rewriting
Quarterly phishing simulation campaigns with mandatory remedial training
DMARC, DKIM, and SPF enforcement on all corporate email domains
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy AI-powered email analysis, implement voice call verification for high-value transactions, and establish social engineering red team programme.

risks.title: Payment System Fraud
risks.description: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in payment processing systems (RENTAS, FPX, DuitNow) or card networks leading to unauthorized transactions or financial losses.
existingControls: Real-time transaction monitoring with rule-based and ML fraud detection
PCI DSS compliance for all card payment processing environments
Velocity checks and transaction limits with dynamic risk-based adjustments
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement behavioural biometrics for payment authentication, deploy network-level fraud analytics, and enhance real-time transaction scoring models.
